images.alt: Official Arrowhead Country Club photo gallery — three separate galleries for The Cypress, The Lakes, and The Waterway nines
images.caption: The club's official media page — three click-to-enlarge galleries organized by nine (Cypress / Lakes / Waterway). The single best primary source for full-bleed hero imagery of all 27 holes.
images.rights_note: Official club marketing imagery. Editorial-use permission via Arrowhead's office (arrow@sccoast.net / 843-236-3243). Direct image URLs are gated behind a JS gallery — request high-res files from the club.

images.alt: Arrowhead Country Club 27-hole course gallery — Cypress, Lakes, and Waterway, with a photo for every hole 1-9 on each nine
images.caption: Arrowhead's tee-times partner site hosts a complete hole-by-hole gallery for all 27 holes — the cleanest single source if you need to anchor every hole spread in the book with a verified image.
images.source: Arrowhead Country Club Myrtle Beach (official partner site)
images.attribution: Courtesy of Arrowhead Country Club / Myrtle Beach Golf Trips
images.rights_note: Affiliated booking partner; imagery sourced from the club. Permission via Arrowhead marketing.

images.alt: Aerial photo tour of Arrowhead's Waterway nine, including the holes leading up to the Intracoastal Waterway
images.caption: Play Golf Myrtle Beach's Jim Maggio drone tour of the Waterway nine — captured on a 'picture-perfect afternoon' along the Intracoastal Waterway in January 2026. Aerials of holes leading up to and including the signature 5th.
images.source: Play Golf Myrtle Beach (official Myrtle Beach Area Golf Holiday Association)
images.attribution: Jim Maggio / Play Golf Myrtle Beach
images.rights_note: Play Golf Myrtle Beach (Myrtle Beach Area Golf Holiday Association) marketing imagery. Editorial reuse via PGMB with credit to Jim Maggio.

images.alt: Photo Gallery: Arrowhead Country Club — 18-photo album showcasing the 4.5-star course
images.caption: Play Golf Myrtle Beach's curated 2017 photo gallery — 18 frames showing the course at peak conditioning, suitable for atmospheric/landscape spreads.
images.attribution: Play Golf Myrtle Beach marketing team
images.rights_note: PGMB marketing imagery. Editorial reuse with credit.

images.alt: Current playing conditions at Arrowhead Country Club — David Williams photo set, June 2025
images.caption: David Williams' ground-level photo set from a June 2025 visit to Arrowhead — current-conditions imagery showing the course mid-summer with mature MiniVerde greens.
images.attribution: David Williams / Play Golf Myrtle Beach
images.rights_note: PGMB marketing imagery. Editorial reuse via PGMB with credit.

images.alt: Arrowhead Country Club feature with hero imagery emphasizing the 27 holes along the Intracoastal
images.caption: Myrtle Beach Golf Channel's editorial feature — '27 Holes of Championship-Caliber Golf Along the Intracoastal' — opens with a wide property frame and intersperses hole-level imagery.
images.rights_note: Editorial photography. Contact site editorial team for reuse rights.

images.alt: Arrowhead Cypress nine hole-by-hole imagery, including holes 2, 3, and 4 — described as three of the most scenic holes in America
images.caption: Official Cypress nine page with photos for every hole. Cypress 2 (571-yard par 5, the 'Beast'), Cypress 3 (par 3, 3-handicap, tee shot over water and bunker), and Cypress 4 (drivable par 4 over a creek fed by the Intracoastal) are the signature trio.
images.rights_note: Official club content. Editorial use via club marketing.

images.alt: Arrowhead Waterway nine hole-by-hole imagery — featuring the par 4 5th along the Intracoastal Waterway
images.caption: Official Waterway nine page. The 5th (407 yards from the tips, 387 from whites) plays along the Intracoastal from tee to green and is widely considered the course's signature hole. The 3rd (175-yard par 3) plays to a virtual island green.
images.rights_note: Official club content. Editorial use via club marketing.

images.alt: Arrowhead Lakes nine hole-by-hole imagery — featuring the par 5 third with a bulkheaded green protected by water
images.caption: Official Lakes nine page. Lakes 3 (587-yard par 5) is the headliner — a bulkheaded green protected by water on the front, classic Floyd risk-reward. The short par-3 5th and risk-reward par-5 9th round out the photogenic moments.
images.rights_note: Official club content. Editorial use via club marketing.

images.alt: GolfPass / Golf Advisor reviewer photos of Arrowhead's Cypress and Waterway nines, including hole 4 on Cypress
images.caption: GolfPass reviewer photo set with multiple verified Arrowhead frames — most notably 'The fourth hole on Arrowhead's Cypress nine is one of the more interesting holes on the property' (TimGavrichGP, Nov 2018). Tim Gavrich is a GolfPass senior writer — a usable editorial source.
images.attribution: Tim Gavrich / GolfPass (and other user-contributed reviewers ChrisW5226, JackStraw6, dshot22)
images.rights_note: Mixed rights. Gavrich's frames are GolfPass editorial — license via GolfPass. User-submitted photos require permission from the individual contributors.

images.alt: Video and stills of Arrowhead's key holes — Waterway 3 and 5, Cypress 3 and 4, Lakes 7 and 9
images.caption: David Williams' 'Key Holes in Myrtle Beach Golf' reel covering the marquee two holes on each Arrowhead nine — exactly the holes the book wants to feature. Stills can be pulled from the embedded video for editorial reference.
images.attribution: David Williams / Play Golf Myrtle Beach
images.rights_note: PGMB video reel. License stills via PGMB with credit.

images.alt: Aerial video spotlight on Arrowhead Country Club — five holes along the 23-mile Intracoastal Waterway stretch
images.caption: Drone video tour, June 2025 — 'five holes on the Intracoastal Waterway' including the historic 23-mile stretch that connected Massachusetts to Key West in 1936. Strong source for hero aerials over the property.
images.attribution: Play Golf Myrtle Beach drone team
images.rights_note: PGMB video. License stills via PGMB with credit.

images.alt: Arrowhead Country Club editorial review with course photography
images.caption: Myrtle Beach Golf Authority review — extensive editorial photography including the Cypress 3 par 3 ('Par 3 with a 3 Handicap'), Lakes 3 par 5, and Waterway 7 holes.
images.attribution: Myrtle Beach Golf Authority / Elliott Realty
images.rights_note: Editorial photography. Contact MB Golf Authority for reuse rights.

images.alt: Golf Course Gurus review with individual hole portraits — Waterway 3 (island green par 3), Lakes 5, Waterway 5, Lakes 9, Waterway 2, Lakes 7
images.caption: Billy Satterfield's editorial review with seven captioned hole portraits — including the 3rd (Waterway) Hole at Arrowhead Country Club (175 Yard Par 3) — the 'virtual island green' the signature designation often refers to.
images.rights_note: Editorial photography. Contact Golf Course Gurus for reuse rights.

images.alt: Arrowhead Country Club listing on the official Visit Myrtle Beach tourism site
images.caption: Visit Myrtle Beach (the destination marketing organization) listing hero — typically a wide property/landscape shot suitable for an opening spread. Tourism-board imagery is commonly licensable for editorial use.
images.source: Visit Myrtle Beach (Myrtle Beach Area Chamber of Commerce)
images.attribution: Visit Myrtle Beach / Myrtle Beach Area CVB
images.rights_note: Destination marketing imagery; typically licensable for editorial use with credit. Contact the CVB media office.

images.alt: Golf Digest course profile — Arrowhead Country Club: Lakes/Waterway/Cypress
images.caption: Golf Digest's course profile (Places to Play). Carries an editorial hero image and the official four-star rating. Useful as a third-party-credibility frame for the editorial intro pages.
images.rights_note: Editorial photography. License via Golf Digest / Discovery licensing.

research_notes: Arrowhead is a 1994 Raymond Floyd + Tom Jackson 27-hole design (Cypress, Lakes, Waterway) at 1201 Burcale Rd, Myrtle Beach, SC 29579. Verified across the club's own site (arrowheadcc.com/media + /cypress, /lakes, /waterway), the partner Myrtle Beach Golf Trips site (arrowheadccmyrtlebeach.com/course-gallery), and editorial coverage from Play Golf Myrtle Beach, GolfPass, GolfDigest, Myrtle Beach Golf Authority, and Golf Course Gurus. The marquee scenic holes per multiple verified sources: Waterway 5 (387-yard par 4 along the Intracoastal — the official 'signature' hole per the club and PGMB), Waterway 3 (175-yard par 3, virtual island green — Golf Course Gurus' 'signature hole'), Cypress 2 (571-yard 'Beast' par 5 — Eddie Dennis's pick), Cypress 3 (par 3 with #3 handicap, water carry — described as one of three scenic holes 'in America'), Cypress 4 (drivable par 4 over creek fed by Intracoastal — TimGavrichGP/GolfPass and Aquaman Trail feature), Lakes 3 (587-yard par 5 with bulkheaded green — Eddie Dennis's Lakes pick), and Lakes 9 (521-yard par 5 risk/reward closer). DIRECT IMAGE URL LIMITATION: The official site and most editorial pages use JavaScript galleries that don't expose direct .jpg/.webp URLs to crawlers. Sources are therefore listed as canonical PAGE URLs (matching the pattern used in true-blue.json). For production hero imagery, contact (1) Arrowhead Country Club marketing at arrow@sccoast.net / 843-236-3243, (2) Play Golf Myrtle Beach / Jim Maggio for drone aerials, and (3) GolfPass editorial for Tim Gavrich's frames. Photographer Jim Maggio and PGMB's David Williams are the most consistent named attribution for current-conditions and aerial work. NOTE ON ADDRESS: Golf Digest shows '1201 Burcal Rd' (typo) and the club itself uses both '29577' (mailing/older) and '29579' (current ZIP) — 1201 Burcale Rd, Myrtle Beach, SC 29579 is the verified current address.
